USUSDT.P.proposed_type: Proposed Wait / Parabolic - No Base Yet
USUSDT.P.bias: REGIME CHANGE (2026-07-15, live TradingView, wall-clock confirmed): the short thesis is fully dead. Since the last check (~0.0252, no approach to the zone), price has exploded through the ENTIRE 0.027-0.0309 structure - the original entry zone, the reclaim line, and the SL - in one violent move, now trading ~0.0332 (+22% today per the live ticker, matches Bybit's independent +23% gainer-list read). The card's own Live row confirms: SL TAGGED, Jul 15 07:30. This is not a rejection-then-reclaim sequence, it is a single explosive parabolic candle blowing straight through the old resistance cluster with no pause. Per the Parabolic Spike Rule, this rules out any blind long chase right now regardless of how bullish the reclaim looks - no base has formed yet. The old resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300) is now a candidate support zone if it gets retested and holds, but that hasn't happened yet either.
USUSDT.P.btc_eth_filter: Aligned bullish, but nowhere close to the driver. BTC +3.37% ($64,681), ETH +5.24% ($1,878.65) per Bybit today - both green, but US's own +22% move is roughly 4-7x the majors' move. This is coin-specific, not beta.
USUSDT.P.catalyst: Unverified. No US-specific news checked; a move this size without a confirmed catalyst is itself a flag - treat as News Risk-adjacent until checked, not Clear.

entry.basis: No valid entry yet. Direction has flipped from short to long-biased (the reclaim is real and the SL tagged), but the Parabolic Spike Rule blocks any entry until either (a) a base forms after this spike, or (b) price pulls back and retests the old 0.0286-0.0300 resistance cluster from above as new support, with a confirmed rejection close (not just a wick) holding it. Neither has happened yet - this is a fresh, unmapped structure as of this read.

USUSDT.P.invalidation: N/A - no live entry to invalidate. Watch for: a base forming (multi-candle consolidation, not one candle), or a retest of 0.0286-0.0300 that holds as support (bullish continuation) vs. fails and closes back below (bull trap / fade back into range).
USUSDT.P.nearest_liquidity: The former resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300, the same one this ledger tracked for two days as a short target) is the first real structure below current price and the most likely retest zone if this pulls back. Below that, 0.0270 (yesterday's rejection zone) and the 0.0217 base. Above current price is untested air - no prior structure, since this is a fresh high.
USUSDT.P.reclaim_status: Reclaimed decisively: price closed through the entire 0.027-0.0309 structure and is holding well above it (~0.0332 vs. 0.0309 SL line). This is a genuine reclaim, not a wick - the old resistance cluster's role has flipped and it is now a candidate support zone, not proven yet since it hasn't been retested from above.
USUSDT.P.relative_strength: Extreme outperformer - +22% today against a market that's up single digits (BTC +3.37%, ETH +5.24%). This is real coin-specific strength, not correlation, which raises the stakes on the unverified-catalyst flag above - big relative moves like this are usually catalyst-driven even when the catalyst hasn't surfaced on TradingView yet.
USUSDT.P.final_action: WAIT / NO CHASE - Parabolic Spike Rule applies. Do not chase the move. Watch for a base or a held retest of 0.0286-0.0300 before considering any long entry.
USUSDT.P.notes: 2026-07-14 fresh reanalysis (supersedes the dead 07-07 origin-base long thesis - TP3 already tagged Jul 9 per the on-chart card, journaled below). TradingView hit its known interval-switch render bug repeatedly this session; SL widened after review from 0.0296 to 0.0309 to clear the real 07-09 19:00-07-10 07:00 supply cluster (true top 0.0300, not the understated 0.0285-0.0288). Correct short convention (entry low, 0.027) put TP1 at 1.36R, below the 2R floor - setup was WAIT/SKIP even on a clean rejection. 2026-07-14 correction: the 11:00 candle was a valid single-candle rejection (close 0.026272, fully below the 0.027 floor), not merely a touch - reasoning fixed, call unchanged (still WAIT/SKIP, R:R floor was the real blocker). 2026-07-15 10:33 UTC full regime-change reanalysis: the short thesis is dead. Price broke through the entire zone structure and the SL level in one parabolic move (~0.026 to ~0.033, +22% today), confirmed both on the live Pine card (SL TAGGED, Jul 15 07:30) and the raw candles (one violent breakout candle, no pause). Direction flipped to long-biased per the reclaim, but Parabolic Spike Rule blocks any entry until a base forms or the old resistance cluster (0.0286-0.0300) gets retested from above and holds. Cleared entry/SL/TP fields rather than guess at a level with zero structure to back it - this is intentionally a 'no clean setup yet' record, not a placeholder trade.

JTOUSDT.P.bias: Fresh 07-14 16:47 UTC+1 read (live TradingView, wall-clock verified): since the last check, price staged a SECOND test of the entry zone - rallied hard from the 0.6095 area, wicked slightly ABOVE 0.68 (the reclaim line) on one 1H candle, but that candle's body/close stayed below 0.68 - a wick-only sweep, not a confirmed reclaim. Price then rejected hard back down to a higher low (~0.615, above the original 0.6095 low) and has since climbed back to consolidate around 0.65. Net effect: the zone has now been tested and held TWICE (the original Jul 13 rejection, and this stronger above-0.68-wick rejection) - reassuring for the thesis, but per the mitigation discussion, a THIRD test would carry more doubt than the first two.
JTOUSDT.P.btc_eth_filter: Not re-checked this read; prior context was aligned bearish (BTC/ETH both red alongside JTO's failed breakout).

entry.basis: Retest of the same 0.665-0.680 shelf that just failed as a reclaim, approached from below this time. JTOUSDT.P.invalidation: A 1H close back above 0.695 (clearing the 0.6772 sweep wick with real buffer, not just the bare zone edge) invalidates the failed-reclaim short.
JTOUSDT.P.nearest_liquidity: Buy-side above 0.685, then the 0.6772 sweep high itself; sell-side: two distinct demand zones below current price, user-drawn and verified - Zone 1 0.6076 (top) to 0.5991 (bottom), Zone 2 0.5934 (top) to 0.5806 (bottom), plus a deeper base zone visually read (not user-verified) at roughly 0.545 (top) to 0.52 (bottom). Every TP now sits at its zone's NEAR edge per the fully-sharpened TP Zone Validity Rule: TP1 0.6076 (Zone 1 top, no precondition), TP2 0.5934 (Zone 2 top, real target only once Zone 1 confirms broken), TP3 ~0.545 (deeper base top, real target only once Zone 2 also confirms broken - and this boundary is approximate, needs a precise chart re-check).
JTOUSDT.P.reclaim_status: Held for a second time (as of the prior read): wicked above 0.68, closed back below it. Since then price broke DOWN decisively - fell straight through the 0.65 consolidation and the ~0.605-0.615 higher-low support, currently trading around 0.6193 (as of this read, 2026-07-15 08:23 UTC). No reclaim attempt in this leg; this is clean continuation in the short's favor.
JTOUSDT.P.relative_strength: Was strong (+10% vs red BTC/ETH) but the breakout attempt failed under its own weight; now declining in line with a red broader market, not against it. BTC/ETH not re-verified this specific pass.
JTOUSDT.P.final_action: HOLD - thesis working well, unrealized profit now ~1.5R and closing in on the corrected TP1 (0.6076, Zone 1's near edge). Still no structural reason to move SL to breakeven pre-TP1. TP2 (0.5934) and TP3 (~0.545) are conditional - only real targets once the zone before each is confirmed broken by a held close, not before.
JTOUSDT.P.notes: 2026-07-13 fresh re-analysis, SL widened after review: 0.685 was too tight (only 0.0078 above the actual 0.6772 sweep wick, inside that single candle's own ~0.0227 range). Widened to 0.695 for real buffer against JTO's elevated realized volatility today. R:R recomputed at the wider SL; still passes both floors. 2026-07-14 09:00 fresh re-analysis: ledger position_status/entry.actual corrected to reflect the live entered trade (was stale at NO POSITION/null); price has consolidated 0.63-0.65 for the last several hours after sweeping to 0.6095 - no new signal, trade still working, no management change recommended. 2026-07-14 16:47 UTC+1 follow-up (after a browser-tooling outage delayed the check by ~2 hours real time, confirmed via `date -u`): live TradingView re-check shows the zone survived a second, stronger test (wick above 0.68, closed below). No management change. 2026-07-15 08:23 UTC follow-up (after a second, longer gap - session tab reset, real time had moved ~16.5 hours, confirmed via `date -u`; also hit the interval/symbol-switch render bug again on the fresh tab, cleared via reload): price has broken down hard since the last check, from ~0.65 through the ~0.605-0.615 support to ~0.6193 now. Unrealized profit up to ~1.5R from ~0.47R. 2026-07-15 (same pass, immediately after) TP1 corrected per the new TP Zone Validity Rule: user pointed out the old TP1 (0.59) sat inside a second demand zone (0.5934-0.5806), skipping past an untested first zone (0.6076-0.5991) entirely. Corrected TP1 to 0.5968 (just past Zone 1's far edge, before Zone 2). Verified TP2/TP3 visually against the same rule - no violation found, but with lower confidence than TP1 since those boundaries weren't user-provided. 2026-07-15 second TP1 correction after user review: rule sharpened - TP1 belongs at the NEAR edge of the first opposing zone (0.6076, Zone 1 top), not past its far edge; even traversing zone 1 was an untested assumption. At 0.6076 this trade pays 1.91R, BELOW the 2R Intraday floor - under the fully-corrected rulebook this setup would have been WAIT/SKIP at analysis time. Trade is live and in profit, so this is managed forward (take the partial at 0.6076), but journal the lesson: map zone boundaries BEFORE placing SL/TP; every correction this week moved R:R down, never up. 2026-07-15 third TP correction, user-prompted: TP2 and TP3 must follow the same near-edge rule as TP1, not just TP1 alone. TP2 moved to 0.5934 (Zone 2 top, was 0.56 - the old value assumed clearing Zone 2 entirely, same untested-assumption error just fixed on TP1). TP3 moved to ~0.545 (deeper base top, was 0.52 - same fix, lower confidence since that zone's boundary was only visually read, not user-verified). Both TP2 and TP3 are conditional targets: TP2 only becomes real once Zone 1 (0.5991-0.6076) is confirmed broken by a held close; TP3 only once Zone 2 is also confirmed broken. R:R at each: TP1 1.91R, TP2 2.39R, TP3 ~4.0R (approximate, pending precise TP3 zone verification).

journal.result: Chart's 'AutoTrader Trade Map' script source was corrupted to a garbage string, with two stacked broken instances on the chart. Not a trading mistake; APE's setup thesis was unaffected and rendered correctly once fixed.
journal.note: Recovery: reload the chart tab, reopen Pine Editor, select-all + paste correct script via a real paste event (clipboard write + native paste, or a synthetic ClipboardEvent dispatched on the Monaco .inputarea if OS clipboard permission is unavailable), save, then explicitly click Add to chart (saving alone does not reattach a removed/broken instance). Verify by reading the rendered card, not by absence of the compile-error icon alone.
